title: New questions over Epstein documents which have not been released by DOJ
fullText: Cuba says 4 people killed after gunfight with Florida speedboat01:56Cars sucked into sinkhole at Omaha intersection01:08FBI raids properties of Los Angeles schools chief01:27Now PlayingUP NEXTFourth grade class surprises student with uplifting messages01:16Trump focuses on economy in State of Union speech02:21Investigators return to Nancy Guthrie’s home01:29Teen who lost leg to cancer scores long-awaited basket01:30Trump set for State of the Union speech in primetime04:02New risks from historic blizzard01:55Savannah Guthrie announces $1 million reward for any information leading to her mother03:03Lawsuit alleges insurance company provider list is a “Ghost Network”03:31Texas Congressman faces new calls to resign after texts revealed01:34Record setting blizzard slams Northeast02:39Blizzard causes massive power outages in New Jersey00:55President Trump reacts after alarming security incident at Mar-a-Lago01:33More than 30 inches of snow in parts of Massachusetts00:56American tourists scramble amid cartel violence in Mexico01:45Historic blizzard grounds nearly all air travel in Northeast01:00Measles cases near 1,000 since start of 202601:29Nightly NewsCuba says 4 people killed after gunfight with Florida speedboat01:56Cars sucked into sinkhole at Omaha intersection01:08FBI raids properties of Los Angeles schools chief01:27Now PlayingNew questions over Epstein documents which have not been released by DOJ02:14UP NEXTFourth grade class surprises student with uplifting messages01:16Trump focuses on economy in State of Union speech02:21
summary: The Justice Department has not released summaries and notes from some FBI interviews with a woman who alleges she was sexually assaulted by both Jeffrey Epstein and President Trump, according to an NBC News analysis and information provided by a source familiar with the investigation. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson reports.
